topic: Someone receives an invitation to their high school reunion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The envelope felt heavy in Maya’s hands, the thick cardstock a stark contrast to the casual clutter of her desk. Her breath hitched as she scanned the elegant script: "You are cordially invited to the Class of 2005 Reunion." A cold knot tightened in her stomach. She reread the date, the location, the sheer audacity of the invitation. Her fingers started to tremble, the paper crinkling under the pressure. Who would even send this? She quickly shoved the mail under a stack of bills, her heart thumping a frantic rhythm against her ribs. Every instinct screamed at her to burn it, to destroy the evidence of this unexpected summons.

[story 2]

Liam stared at the embossed invitation, the glossy paper reflecting the harsh overhead light of his apartment. He traced the looping calligraphy with a fingertip, his knuckles turning white. "A reunion?" he muttered to the empty room, the sound rough in his throat. He stood up abruptly, pacing the length of his living room twice before stopping dead by the window. Outside, the city lights blurred into streaks of indifferent color. A wave of profound unease washed over him, chilling him despite the warmth of the room. He felt a sudden, desperate need to check every lock, every window, as if some unseen threat was lurking just beyond the glass.

[story 3]

Sarah picked up the invitation as if it were contaminated. The crisp edges felt unnervingly sharp. She held it up to the light, scrutinizing the details—the font, the seal, the specific phrasing. A sudden dryness coated her mouth. She dropped the card onto the floor, the sound surprisingly loud in the quiet hallway. Her gaze darted around the room, scanning the shadows pooled in the corners. A tightness constricted her chest, making each inhale shallow and strained. She began methodically gathering every item on her nightstand, stacking them with deliberate, jerky movements, seeking some semblance of control in the face of this unwelcome notification.
